original_abstract: Drawing on an exhaustive survey of global referendums dating back to 1793, Dr. Qvortrup and his contributors offer a comprehensive analysis of the historical drivers and political circumstances that lead governments to put policy issues directly to the people. *Referendums Around the World* examines the evolving role of direct democracy, exploring why and when citizens are invited to vote on critical legislative and constitutional matters. By synthesizing over two centuries of data, the authors provide a vital account of the mechanisms behind public consultation and the shifting landscape of popular sovereignty.
humanized_abstract: Referendums: From Rigged To Raw Covers a whole range of referendums held across the world since 1793 (an enormous survey has been conducted for the purpose of this book). For the first time in the historiography of direct democracy it is possible to give a full and academically informed account of what has triggered referendums through time, and why and when policies and constitutional issues have been submitted to the peoples’ veto. Thus Referendums Around the World examines from a historical as well as political perspective the role that referendums may have played over time. To provide a definitive account of what has been happening across the world over time is, by any standards, vital to any understanding of citizens being allowed to make decisions through the democratic ballot box, and thereby a study of referendums enables us to examine an understanding of popular democracy.
